reviews.author: Oliver Sang
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the obj_stock_pcp structure has been modified to fit within a single 64-byte cache line on non-debug 64-bit builds, but this change may not be sufficient as it only accounts for one CPU consuming or refilling against five different objcgs, and requested further investigation into the impact of multiple CPUs accessing the same slot.

Reviewer noted that the patch v2 (8da1b1ea43) can recover performance when applied on top of 01b9da291c, but since it's out-of-date, they won't provide a full comparison.

Reviewer Oliver Sang noted that the patch is significantly different from v2 or v3 and requested confirmation on whether to test v3 instead.
reviews.raw_body:
hi, Shakeel,

On Mon, May 18, 2026 at 10:54:20AM -0700, Shakeel Butt wrote:

---

firstly, let me still give you an update about v2. I applied it directly on top
of 01b9da291c, found it can recover the performance.

=========================================================================================
compiler/cpufreq_governor/kconfig/method/nr_threads/rootfs/tbox_group/test/testcase/testtime:
  gcc-14/performance/x86_64-rhel-9.4/mq/100%/debian-13-x86_64-20250902.cgz/lkp-spr-r02/switch/stress-ng/60s

commit: 
  8285917d6f ("mm: memcontrol: prepare for reparenting non-hierarchical stats")
  01b9da291c ("mm: memcontrol: convert objcg to be per-memcg per-node type")
  8da1b1ea43 ("memcg: cache obj_stock by memcg, not by objcg pointer")   <---- v2

8285917d6f383aef 01b9da291c4969354807b52956f 8da1b1ea4344c152a3892cbb132
---------------- --------------------------- ---------------------------
         %stddev     %change         %stddev     %change         %stddev
             \          |                \          |                \
      5849          +210.2%      18145   3%      +0.8%       5896        stress-ng.switch.nanosecs_per_context_switch_mq_method
 2.296e+09           -67.7%  7.408e+08   3%      -0.8%  2.278e+09        stress-ng.switch.ops
  38288993           -67.7%   12355813   3%      -0.8%   37987427        stress-ng.switch.ops_per_sec

but since this version is out-of-date now, I won't give out the full
comparison. if you still want it, please let me know.

---

got it. will change to test following patch. and this looks quite different
with v2 or v3, so if you still want us to test v3, please let me know. thanks!

2026-05-19.patch_summary: This patch addresses a regression in the memcg (memory cgroup) code, specifically related to the obj_stock_pcp structure and its handling of object caching. The patch modifies the obj_stock_pcp structure to cache multiple obj_cgroups instead of just one, which improves performance by reducing cache misses. It also introduces a new NR_OBJ_STOCK define that determines the number of cached obj_cgroups. Additionally, the patch includes changes to the obj_stock_pcp initialization and locking mechanisms to support the new caching behavior.
